filter_reason: Agent memory for long-horizon tasks is a live orchestration problem, and this proposes a proactive memory architecture rather than another passive retrieval store.
summary: This paper tackles a core failure mode of long-running agents: as trajectories grow, task requirements, prior attempts, and open subgoals get buried in or pushed out of the context window and stop influencing decisions. It proposes a proactive memory agent that surfaces decision-relevant state at the moment it matters, instead of waiting for the action agent to query for it. Memory management is one of the main bottlenecks for long-horizon agent reliability, so approaches like this are directly relevant to anyone building agents that run for hours or days.
title: Remember When It Matters: Proactive Memory Agent for Long-Horizon Agents
excerpt: In long-horizon tasks, decision-relevant state is often scattered across an expanding trajectory, while the action agent must surface it and act. As trajectories grow, task requirements, environment facts, prior attempts, diagnoses, and open subgoals can be buried in the context window or pushed beyond it, failing to influence decisions when needed. We call this failure mode "behavioral state decay". We study memory as an active intervention mechanism rather than passive retrieval.
